title: Upgrade Parent Dashboard Live Chat with Real-Time Messaging Features

requirements.text: Replace the existing localStorage-based chat in ParentDashboard.tsx and StudentMessagesPage.tsx with a polling-free real-time chat system. Use the Internet Computer backend (Motoko) to store messages in stable state with a timestamp, senderId, senderRole (parent/student), messageText, and a read flag. Expose query and update functions: sendMessage, getMessages, and markMessagesRead.

quotes: Store all messages in a cloud database.
Messages should appear immediately for both sender and receiver.

acceptanceCriteria: Messages are persisted in stable Motoko state and survive canister upgrades.
sendMessage stores a new message with correct sender, role, text, and ISO timestamp.
getMessages returns all messages for a given parent–student pair in chronological order.
markMessagesRead marks all unread messages from the other party as read.

requirements.text: Implement a React Query polling loop (1-second interval) in both ParentDashboard.tsx and StudentMessagesPage.tsx to simulate real-time message delivery by refetching getMessages frequently, giving the appearance of instant updates without a page refresh.

quotes: Use WebSockets or Firebase Realtime Database for instant messaging (no page refresh required).
Messages should appear immediately for both sender and receiver.

acceptanceCriteria: New messages sent by one party appear on the other party's screen within 2 seconds without any manual page refresh.
Polling starts when the chat section is mounted and stops when it unmounts.

requirements.text: Add online/offline presence tracking. Store a lastSeen timestamp and isOnline boolean per user in Motoko stable state. Expose updatePresence and getPresence functions. Call updatePresence on a regular interval (every 30 seconds) from the frontend. Display a green dot for online (seen within 60 seconds) and a grey dot with 'Last seen' time for offline users in the chat header.
acceptanceCriteria: A green indicator and 'Online' label appear when the other party has been active within 60 seconds.
A grey indicator and 'Last seen HH:MM' appear otherwise.
Presence updates are sent to the backend every 30 seconds while the chat is open.

requirements.text: Add a typing indicator. Store a isTyping boolean and typingUserId in component state. When the local user types in the input field, broadcast a setTyping(true) flag via a Motoko update call and clear it after 3 seconds of inactivity. Poll getTypingStatus on a 1-second interval and display 'User is typing...' with an animated ellipsis below the message list when the remote party is typing.
acceptanceCriteria: Typing indicator appears within 1 second of the remote party starting to type.
Typing indicator disappears within 4 seconds after the remote party stops typing.
The animated ellipsis is visible and styled to match the chat bubble design.

requirements.text: Display a formatted timestamp (e.g., '10:32 AM') beneath each chat bubble. Group messages by date with a centered date separator (e.g., 'Today', 'Yesterday', 'Dec 12'). Auto-scroll the message list to the latest message whenever new messages arrive or a message is sent.
acceptanceCriteria: Each message displays a timestamp formatted as HH:MM AM/PM.
Date separators appear between message groups of different calendar days.
The message container scrolls to the bottom automatically on new messages and on initial load.

requirements.text: Redesign the chat UI in ParentDashboard.tsx and StudentMessagesPage.tsx with modern chat bubbles: outgoing messages aligned right with a primary colour background, incoming messages aligned left with a neutral background, rounded corners, sender avatar initials, and a clean input bar with a send button. Ensure the layout is fully mobile responsive with the message list taking available vertical space and the input bar pinned to the bottom.

quotes: Use modern UI with clean chat bubbles.
Make it mobile responsive and optimized for PWA.

acceptanceCriteria: Outgoing bubbles are right-aligned with the app's primary colour.
Incoming bubbles are left-aligned with a contrasting neutral colour.
The chat input bar is always visible at the bottom of the chat pane on all screen sizes.
The UI renders correctly on 375px width (mobile) and 1280px width (desktop).

requirements.text: Show an in-app notification toast (using the existing toast provider) when a new message arrives while the user is on a different page or the chat panel is not in focus. Use the existing browser Notification API (already configured in the service worker) to send a push notification with the sender's name and message preview when the document is hidden.

quotes: Add push notifications when a new message arrives.

acceptanceCriteria: A toast notification appears when a new message arrives and the chat panel is not active.
A browser push notification fires when the document.visibilityState is 'hidden' and the user has granted notification permission.
Clicking the notification navigates the user to the chat section.

requirements.text: Add basic network error handling in the chat components. Show a non-blocking banner ('Connection lost – retrying…') when backend calls fail due to network issues. Automatically retry failed message sends up to 3 times with exponential backoff. Display a persistent error state if all retries fail, with a manual 'Retry' button.

quotes: Add basic error handling for network disconnection.

acceptanceCriteria: A 'Connection lost' banner appears when a backend call fails.
Failed message sends are retried up to 3 times automatically.
After 3 failures, a visible error state with a 'Retry' button is shown.
The banner dismisses automatically when connectivity is restored and a request succeeds.

requirements.text: Update the unread message badge in Layout.tsx to reflect the real unread message count fetched from the Motoko backend via the getMessages and markMessagesRead functions, replacing the current localStorage-based unread count logic.

quotes: Messages should appear immediately for both sender and receiver.

acceptanceCriteria: The unread badge in the sidebar/nav reflects the backend unread count.
The badge clears when the user opens and views the chat.

constraints: Do not use Firebase, WebSockets, or any external real-time database — use the Internet Computer Motoko backend for all data persistence.
Do not edit any files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
All Motoko logic must remain in the single actor in backend/main.mo.
Real-time behaviour must be achieved via React Query polling, not native WebSockets or third-party services.
nonGoals: Firebase Authentication, Firebase Realtime Database, or Firestore integration.
File or image attachments in chat.
PWA manifest or service worker changes beyond triggering browser notifications.
